sections.title: Why Generics Deep Dive matters
sections.content:
Generics prevent runtime ClassCast issues by shifting type checks to compile time.

Day 28 is an inflection point between writing code that "works" and writing code that scales in maintainability and production reliability.

Interviewers at this stage evaluate:
1. Internal understanding (how it works, not only syntax)
2. Trade-off reasoning (why this approach over alternatives)
3. Runtime impact (performance, memory, contention, failure behavior)

This topic should be explained with one minimal example, one realistic failure mode, and one mitigation strategy.

If you can defend your design choices clearly, you will stand out from syntax-only answers.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Generics Deep Dive
sections.content:
### Day 28: Generics Deep Dive
Generics prevent runtime ClassCast issues by shifting type checks to compile time.

### Deep understanding goal
You should understand the mechanism, its boundaries, and when to avoid it.

### Practical workflow
1. Implement baseline.
2. Add correctness checks and edge handling.
3. Evaluate runtime cost.
4. Refactor for readability and team adoption.

### Interview quality bar
- Explain what problem it solves.
- Show concise Java example.
- Name one pitfall and one mitigation.
- State complexity/runtime implication.
- Compare with one alternative.

### Production angle
In backend systems, this concept influences throughput, reliability, and debugging speed. A good implementation must support operations, not just local correctness.

### Quick self-check
- Can I explain this in <60 seconds?
- Can I code it from memory?
- Can I describe a failure scenario and fix?

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Using raw types and losing type safety guarantees.
Misunderstanding ? extends vs ? super (PECS rule).
Casting around generics instead of modeling types correctly.
Ignoring type erasure implications at runtime.
Designing APIs that expose unnecessary wildcard complexity.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a generic type parameter?
conceptual.answer: Placeholder like T that becomes concrete at compile time—type safety without casting everywhere.

conceptual.question: Type erasure?
conceptual.answer: Generics compiled to raw types + casts; no T at runtime—limits some checks.

conceptual.question: Bounded type parameters T extends Number?
conceptual.answer: T must be subtype of Number—allows doubleValue etc.

conceptual.question: Wildcard ? extends vs ? super?
conceptual.answer: PECS: producer extends, consumer super—for flexibility and safety.

conceptual.question: Why can’t new T()?
conceptual.answer: Erasure—constructor unknown at runtime—use factories or Class<T>.

conceptual.question: Raw types still allowed?
conceptual.answer: Legacy interop—avoid; unchecked warnings.

conceptual.question: Generic array creation?
conceptual.answer: Illegal new T[]—use reflection Array.newInstance or List<T>.

conceptual.question: Bridge methods?
conceptual.answer: Compiler generates for overriding generic methods—rare debugging topic.

conceptual.question: Type inference var and diamond <>?
conceptual.answer: Reduce verbosity; still compile-time checked.

conceptual.question: Reifiable types?
conceptual.answer: Primitives, raw types, arrays—know generics limitations with arrays.

conceptual.question: What is Class<T> used for?
conceptual.answer: Runtime type token for reflection when erasure hides T.

conceptual.question: Generic methods static <T> T middle(T[] a)?
conceptual.answer: Type parameter on method, not class.

conceptual.question: Covariance in Java arrays vs generics?
conceptual.answer: Arrays covariant (unsafe); generics invariant—different design.

conceptual.question: Kotlin reified (mention)?
conceptual.answer: Different language—inline reified—not in Java.

conceptual.question: Spring BeanFactory getBean(Class<T>) pattern?
conceptual.answer: Passes Class token to recover type after erasure.

codeBased.question: copy(List<? super T> dest, List<? extends T> src) example.
Wildcard capture compile error fix?
codeBased.answer: Helper generic method with explicit type parameter.

codeBased.question: Avoid heap pollution varargs generic array.
instanceof with generic in old code?
codeBased.answer: Raw checks only at runtime—use Class tokens.

codeBased.question: Cannot catch T extends Throwable—why?
codeBased.answer: Erasure conflicts with catch mechanism—use wrappers.

seniorScenario.question: Jackson TypeReference for generic collections?
seniorScenario.answer: Preserves nested generic types for deserialization.

seniorScenario.question: Protobuf/Avro schemas vs Java generics?
seniorScenario.answer: Code generation separate—mapping layer handles types.

seniorScenario.question: API evolution add type parameter to class?
seniorScenario.answer: Binary compatibility challenges—use overloads or new types.

seniorScenario.question: Performance cost of generics?
seniorScenario.answer: Essentially none—erasure to casts; focus elsewhere.

seniorScenario.question: GSON TypeToken runtime generic—how?
seniorScenario.answer: Anonymous subclass preserves super type args via reflection.

seniorScenario.question: Framework proxies and generic signatures?
seniorScenario.answer: Sometimes need Annotation processing or Spring ResolvableType.

wrongAnswers: Generics exist at runtime as reified T in the JVM.
You can create new T() if T has a no-arg constructor.
List<String> is a subtype of List<Object>.
Wildcards ? have no difference from Object in generics.
Type erasure means all type checks are removed at compile time.
Generic methods cannot be static.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a functional interface?
conceptual.answer: Single abstract method interface—target for lambda expressions.

conceptual.question: Lambda vs anonymous class?
conceptual.answer: Lambda lighter syntax; captures effectively final vars; generates invokedynamic.

conceptual.question: What is method reference?
conceptual.answer: String::length shorthand for s->s.length()—four kinds static, instance, ctor, arbitrary.

conceptual.question: What is effectively final?
conceptual.answer: Local variable assigned once—can be captured by lambdas.

conceptual.question: Functional interfaces in java.util.function?
conceptual.answer: Predicate, Function, Consumer, Supplier—common shapes.

conceptual.question: Exception handling in lambdas?
conceptual.answer: Checked exceptions must be handled or wrapped—can’t throw checked without signature allowing.

conceptual.question: Serialization of lambdas?
conceptual.answer: Fragile—avoid unless stable capturing rules understood.

conceptual.question: Default methods interaction?
conceptual.answer: Functional interface can have many defaults if single abstract method remains.

conceptual.question: Composition Predicate.and/or/negate?
conceptual.answer: Combine boolean functions fluently.

conceptual.question: Currying (mention)?
conceptual.answer: Partial application—higher-order functions in FP style.

conceptual.question: Lambda performance vs anonymous?
conceptual.answer: Similar after JIT—avoid premature micro-optimization.

conceptual.question: this in lambda vs anonymous?
conceptual.answer: Lambda uses enclosing this; anonymous has own this for inner class.

conceptual.question: Target typing?
conceptual.answer: Compiler infers functional interface from assignment context.

conceptual.question: @FunctionalInterface purpose?
conceptual.answer: Compiler error if not exactly one abstract method—documentation.

conceptual.question: Parallel streams and lambdas?
conceptual.answer: Side effects dangerous—thread-safe operations required.

codeBased.question: Function<String,Integer> parseInt safe.
codeBased.answer: Wrap parseInt in try or return Optional.

codeBased.question: Stream filter map collect with lambdas.
Predicate.and vs composing two filters in stream?
codeBased.answer: Equivalent for filter; Predicate helpers readable for reuse.

codeBased.question: Function.compose(f,g) order?
codeBased.answer: compose applies argument function first: this.compose(before).

codeBased.question: BiConsumer vs Consumer?
codeBased.answer: BiConsumer takes two args void return—e.g. Map.forEach.

codeBased.question: UnaryOperator in List.replaceAll?
codeBased.answer: List.replaceAll(UnaryOperator) transforms each element in place.

codeBased.question: Supplier<Double> Math::random vs () -> Math.random()?
codeBased.answer: Method ref to static method zero-arg matches Supplier.

codeBased.question: Why Comparator.comparing(Person::getName)?
codeBased.answer: Key extractor Function; avoids manual null-safe compare boilerplate.

codeBased.question: Lambda vs anonymous Runnable bytecode?
codeBased.answer: Lambdas use invokedynamic; anonymous class separate class file.

seniorScenario.question: Framework passes lambdas to threads—thread safety?
seniorScenario.answer: Capture only immutable or thread-safe state.

seniorScenario.question: Debugging lambda stack traces?
seniorScenario.answer: Line numbers and method names sometimes obscure—use named methods if complex.

seniorScenario.question: Kotlin SAM vs Java functional—interop?
seniorScenario.answer: SAM conversion rules in Kotlin for Java APIs.

seniorScenario.question: Reactive APIs functional style—errors?
seniorScenario.answer: Signal propagation, not exceptions through streams casually.

seniorScenario.question: Code coverage on lambda branches?
seniorScenario.answer: May need extract method for meaningful branch coverage names.

seniorScenario.question: Serialization of captured closures in actors?
seniorScenario.answer: Dangerous—keep messages data-only.

wrongAnswers: Lambdas can modify any local variable they read.
Functional interface can have two abstract methods if defaults exist.
Method references always allocate fewer objects than lambdas.
Lambda syntax works in Java 7.
You can throw checked exceptions freely from any lambda.
Anonymous inner class and lambda are identical at runtime always.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Streams API
sections.content:
### Day 30: Streams API
Streams encourage declarative transformations and are powerful when state and side-effects are controlled.

### Java 8+ stream vocabulary (practice each)
- **Source:** `stream()`, `Stream.of`, `IntStream.range`, `Files.lines` (close with try-with-resources).
- **Intermediate:** `filter`, `map`, `flatMap`, `distinct`, `sorted`, `peek` (debug only), `limit`, `skip`, `takeWhile`/`dropWhile` (Java 9+).
- **Terminal:** `forEach`, `collect`, `reduce`, `min`/`max`, `count`, `anyMatch`/`allMatch`/`noneMatch`, `findFirst`/`findAny`.
- **Collectors:** `toList()` (Java 16+) or `toCollection(ArrayList::new)`, `joining`, `groupingBy`, `partitioningBy`, `mapping`, `summarizingInt`, `averagingInt`, `toMap`, `collectingAndThen`.

### Deep understanding goal
You should understand the mechanism, its boundaries, and when to avoid it.

### Practical workflow
1. Implement baseline.
2. Add correctness checks and edge handling.
3. Evaluate runtime cost.
4. Refactor for readability and team adoption.

### Interview quality bar
- Explain what problem it solves.
- Show concise Java example.
- Name one pitfall and one mitigation.
- State complexity/runtime implication.
- Compare with one alternative.

### Production angle
In backend systems, this concept influences throughput, reliability, and debugging speed. A good implementation must support operations, not just local correctness.

### Quick self-check

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: Stream pipeline: filter + map + collect — declarative collection processing.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: groupingBy: partition employees by department string.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: flatMap: one-to-many transform — lines to words.

sections.title: Problem: distinct and sorted
sections.description: distinct uses equals/hashCode; sorted is encounter-order sort (stateful — may buffer).

sections.title: Problem: limit, skip, takeWhile (Java 9+)
sections.description: Pagination pattern with skip+limit; takeWhile short-circuits while predicate holds (ordered streams).

sections.title: Problem: peek — debug only, not business logic
sections.description: peek is for debugging; do not rely on it for required side effects — optimization may elide or reorder.

sections.title: Problem: reduce — sum and combine
sections.description: One-arg reduce returns Optional for empty; two-arg reduce uses identity; combiner used in parallel.

sections.title: Problem: Collectors.joining
sections.description: Delimiter, optional prefix/suffix — builds strings without manual StringBuilder loops.

sections.title: Problem: groupingBy + counting
sections.description: Downstream collector counting() — frequency map pattern for interviews.

sections.title: Problem: groupingBy + mapping + toSet
sections.description: Nested collector: group keys, then map each group to another reduction (unique skills per dept).

sections.title: Problem: partitioningBy
sections.description: Special case of groupingBy for Predicate — always exactly two buckets: true / false.

sections.title: Problem: summarizingInt / averagingDouble
sections.description: IntSummaryStatistics bundles count, sum, min, max, average — one pass per group.

sections.title: Problem: toMap and duplicate keys
sections.description: Third argument merge function resolves key collisions — without it, duplicate key throws IllegalStateException.

sections.title: Problem: anyMatch / allMatch / noneMatch
sections.description: Short-circuiting search — may stop before consuming entire stream (efficient for large sources).

sections.title: Problem: collectingAndThen — finish with unmodifiableList
sections.description: Finisher runs after collection — wrap or transform result (immutable view, first element, etc.).

sections.title: Problem: flatMap — chars and nested lists
sections.description: flatMap flattens Stream<Stream<T>> to Stream<T>; IntStream.chars for primitive char codes.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is the Streams API?
conceptual.answer: Declarative pipeline on data: source, intermediate ops (lazy), terminal op (eager).

conceptual.question: Lazy vs eager?
conceptual.answer: Intermediate ops build pipeline; nothing runs until terminal like collect/count/forEach.

conceptual.question: What is spliterator?
conceptual.answer: Splitting iterator backing parallel streams—correct splitting affects speedup.

conceptual.question: Parallel stream pitfalls?
conceptual.answer: Side effects, order, wrong data structures (LinkedList), fork/join overhead small data.

conceptual.question: map vs flatMap?
conceptual.answer: map 1:1; flatMap one-to-many flattening streams.

conceptual.question: collect Collectors.groupingBy?
conceptual.answer: Downstream collectors for aggregation—powerful but verbose.

conceptual.question: Optional from findFirst?
conceptual.answer: Represents absence safely—avoid get without isPresent or orElse.

conceptual.question: Primitive streams IntStream?
conceptual.answer: Avoid boxing in numeric pipelines—mapToInt etc.

conceptual.question: What is Stream builder?
conceptual.answer: When source not standard collection—Stream.builder().

conceptual.question: Short-circuit operations?
conceptual.answer: anyMatch, findFirst may not process entire stream—lazy benefit.

conceptual.question: Stateful intermediate operations distinct/sorted?
conceptual.answer: May buffer entire stream—memory cost.

conceptual.question: peek for debugging?
conceptual.answer: Side-effect only debugging—not guaranteed to execute if optimized away—don’t rely for logic.

conceptual.question: Iterator vs Stream?
conceptual.answer: Stream not reusable; Iterator imperative—choose per style and performance.

conceptual.question: try-with-resources on streams?
conceptual.answer: Files.lines must be closed—use try-with-resources.

conceptual.question: Custom collector?
conceptual.answer: Collector.of supplier/accumulator/combiner/finisher for domain aggregation.

codeBased.question: parallel stream wrong for LinkedList why?
partitioningBy vs groupingBy(Boolean)?
codeBased.answer: partitioningBy is for Predicate → Map<Boolean,List>; clearer for two buckets.

codeBased.question: Collectors.mapping in nested grouping?
codeBased.answer: groupingBy(key, mapping(field, toSet())) for sets per group.

codeBased.question: Stream.iterate seed UnaryOperator?
codeBased.answer: Stream.iterate(0, n -> n+1) infinite until limit — Java 9+ iterate with hasNext.

codeBased.question: reduce identity vs Optional?
codeBased.answer: Two-arg reduce never empty Optional; one-arg empty stream → Optional.empty().

codeBased.question: Why not collect(Collectors.toMap) without merge on dup keys?
codeBased.answer: IllegalStateException — supply merge function or use groupingBy.

codeBased.question: IntStream.boxed() when?
codeBased.answer: Need Stream<Integer> for collect toList with generic collectors.

codeBased.question: Files.lines Stream close?
codeBased.answer: try (Stream<String> s = Files.lines(path)) { ... } — terminal op closes in try-with-resources pattern.

codeBased.question: sorted(Comparator) stable?
codeBased.answer: TimSort stable for reference types — equal elements keep relative order.

seniorScenario.question: Production OOM on giant Stream distinct?
seniorScenario.answer: Buffered uniqueness or external sort—don’t hold all keys in memory.

seniorScenario.question: Parallel stream in web request—risk?
seniorScenario.answer: Steals common ForkJoinPool—may starve other work; use custom pool.

seniorScenario.question: Database pagination vs Stream of all rows?
seniorScenario.answer: Don’t load entire table into Stream without chunking.

seniorScenario.question: Logging inside stream peek in prod?
seniorScenario.answer: Noise and ordering issues—use proper tracing.

seniorScenario.question: Migration from loops to streams readability?
seniorScenario.answer: Not always clearer—team style guide.

seniorScenario.question: Stream closed twice exception?
seniorScenario.answer: IllegalStateException—streams single-use.

wrongAnswers: Intermediate stream operations execute immediately when called.
Parallel streams always make code faster.
You can reuse a Stream after terminal operation.
peek is intended for business logic side effects.
map and flatMap are identical for collections of collections.
findAny always returns the first element in encounter order.

conceptual.question: What is Optional?
conceptual.answer: Container for absent or present value—avoid null returns as convention.

conceptual.question: When not to use Optional?
conceptual.answer: Fields, parameters, collections of Optionals—often overkill; use nullable with docs.

conceptual.question: orElse vs orElseGet?
conceptual.answer: orElseGet supplier lazy—avoids expensive default if present.

conceptual.question: flatMap in Optional?
conceptual.answer: Chaining possibly empty computations without nested Optionals.

conceptual.question: ifPresent vs map?
conceptual.answer: Side effect vs transform—map returns Optional.

conceptual.question: Optional.get danger?
conceptual.answer: Throws if empty—prefer orElseThrow with message.

conceptual.question: Stream findFirst Optional?
conceptual.answer: Natural fit—empty if no match.

conceptual.question: Optional serialization?
conceptual.answer: Not recommended—Optional not intended as DTO field.

conceptual.question: Kotlin nullable vs Optional?
conceptual.answer: Different idioms—interop awareness.

conceptual.question: API design return Optional?
conceptual.answer: Clearer than null for “no result” methods—document semantics.

conceptual.question: Optional empty singleton?
conceptual.answer: Optional.empty() reused—identity safe.

conceptual.question: filter on Optional?
conceptual.answer: Becomes empty if predicate false—like positive guard.

conceptual.question: Optional of nullable?
conceptual.answer: Optional.ofNullable(x)—empty if null.

conceptual.question: Combining Optionals?
conceptual.answer: map/flatMap chain; or use helper libraries in larger codebases.

conceptual.question: Performance of Optional?
conceptual.answer: Small object overhead—usually negligible vs clarity.

codeBased.question: Chain map flatMap without null checks.
Return first non-empty optional from list—pattern?

seniorScenario.question: JPA repository Optional return?
seniorScenario.answer: Spring Data supports Optional find—align with domain “not found”.

seniorScenario.question: RPC returning Optional across services?
seniorScenario.answer: May not serialize—use explicit response DTO with boolean found.

seniorScenario.question: Reactive Mono Optional anti-pattern?
seniorScenario.answer: Mono already nullable signal—double wrapper confusing.

seniorScenario.question: JSON Optional field Jackson?
seniorScenario.answer: Configure absent vs null carefully—defaults matter.

seniorScenario.question: Team bans Optional in public API—reasons?
seniorScenario.answer: Interop, style—document team rule.

seniorScenario.question: Performance critical path Optional allocation?
seniorScenario.answer: Rarely bottleneck—profile first.

Optional is a primitive type.
Optional.of(null) returns Optional.empty().

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a record?
conceptual.answer: Immutable data carrier with generated accessors, equals, hashCode, toString—less boilerplate.

conceptual.question: Canonical constructor in record?
conceptual.answer: Compact constructor validates without repeating field list.

conceptual.question: Can record implement interfaces?
conceptual.answer: Yes; cannot extend classes (implicitly extends Record).

conceptual.question: Are record components mutable if object mutable?
conceptual.answer: Shallow immutability—don’t expose mutable lists without defensive copy.

conceptual.question: What is sealed class/interface?
conceptual.answer: Permits only listed subclasses—compiler enforced hierarchy.

conceptual.question: Exhaustive switch with sealed?
conceptual.answer: Compiler can verify all cases covered—fewer bugs.

conceptual.question: Pattern matching for instanceof?
conceptual.answer: if (o instanceof Point p) use p directly—Java 16+.

conceptual.question: Record serialization?
conceptual.answer: Works with compatible frameworks—watch evolution adding fields.

conceptual.question: Migration class to record breaking?
conceptual.answer: Constructors and accessors names differ—API breakage.

conceptual.question: Record vs Lombok @Value?
conceptual.answer: Language feature vs annotation processing—trade-offs.

conceptual.question: Accessor naming x() not getX?
conceptual.answer: Record accessors use component names—framework interop considerations.

conceptual.question: sealed permits clause?
conceptual.answer: permits Sub1, Sub2 in same module/package constraints.

conceptual.question: non-sealed subclass?
conceptual.answer: Opt out child can be extended further—escape hatch.

conceptual.question: Record reflection java.lang.Record?
conceptual.answer: Common superclass—limited methods.

conceptual.question: JavaBeans compatibility with records?
conceptual.answer: May need adapters for tools expecting get/set.

codeBased.question: Switch on sealed type exhaustive.
codeBased.answer: Compiler error if case missing (with settings).

codeBased.question: Jackson record module—annotation?
codeBased.answer: Often works out of the box—field names.

seniorScenario.question: Hibernate entity as record?
seniorScenario.answer: Generally not—mutability and proxies; use for DTOs projections.

seniorScenario.question: API versioning add component to record?
seniorScenario.answer: Breaking for clients—use new record type or optional wrapper.

seniorScenario.question: Spring Data projections interface vs record?
seniorScenario.answer: Class-based DTOs + records popular for reads.

seniorScenario.question: Sealed classes and frameworks generating subclasses?
seniorScenario.answer: Permits list must include generated classes—config complexity.

seniorScenario.question: Binary compatibility adding sealed permits?
seniorScenario.answer: Clients recompile—semver major.

seniorScenario.question: Records in public library API stability?
seniorScenario.answer: Great for immutable events—document evolution policy.

wrongAnswers: record can extend another concrete class besides Record.
Record components can be reassigned after construction.
sealed types allow unlimited subclasses in any package.
Records automatically deep-clone mutable fields.
Pattern matching works only with legacy instanceof casts.
sealed is the same as final for a class.

conceptual.question: What is pattern matching for switch?
conceptual.answer: Combine type tests and binding in switch—less casting clutter.

conceptual.question: Switch expression vs statement?
conceptual.answer: Expression yields value; must be exhaustive or default where required.

conceptual.question: Guard clauses in case with when?
conceptual.answer: Additional boolean condition on case labels—Java 21 feature path.

conceptual.question: Record patterns in switch?
conceptual.answer: Deconstruct record components in case labels—reduce accessors.

conceptual.question: Exhaustiveness checking?
conceptual.answer: Compiler ensures all sealed/interface variants handled—safer refactors.

conceptual.question: Null handling in switch?
conceptual.answer: Separate null case or use null-safe dispatch before switch.

conceptual.question: enum switch legacy vs expression?
conceptual.answer: Expression can yield value; break not used with arrows.

conceptual.question: Multiple labels case 1,2,3?
conceptual.answer: Shared body—concise.

conceptual.question: Yield in switch expression?
conceptual.answer: For classic blocks returning value from complex case.

conceptual.question: Pattern dominance errors?
conceptual.answer: Order cases so more specific before general—compiler errors if unreachable.

conceptual.question: Switch on String compiled how?
conceptual.answer: hashCode/equals—null throws NPE.

conceptual.question: Performance of pattern matching?
conceptual.answer: Generally same as equivalent instanceof chain after JIT.

conceptual.question: Preview vs final features?
conceptual.answer: Know your JDK version in interview—enable preview flags in dev only.

conceptual.question: Algebraic data types analogy?
conceptual.answer: Sealed hierarchy + exhaustive switch similar to ADTs in FP.

conceptual.question: Migration from if-else chain to switch?
conceptual.answer: Readability win when dispatch central.

codeBased.question: instanceof pattern matching.
codeBased.answer: if (o instanceof String s) return s.length();

codeBased.question: sealed interface Expr with record cases—switch.
codeBased.answer: return switch(e){ case Add a -> ...; case Num n -> n.v(); };

codeBased.question: String switch with guard when available.
Replace equals chain with pattern switch.
Record pattern case Point(int x, int y)

seniorScenario.question: Library adds sealed subclass—clients switch breaks?
seniorScenario.answer: Exhaustive switch fails compile—good failure vs runtime bug.

seniorScenario.question: Framework reflection on switch generated code?
seniorScenario.answer: Generally same as ordinary calls—watch bytecode differences.

seniorScenario.question: Kotlin when vs Java switch migration?
seniorScenario.answer: Interop patterns in mixed codebases.

seniorScenario.question: Android desugaring new switch features?
seniorScenario.answer: Older API levels—toolchain support considerations.

seniorScenario.question: Performance critical dispatch—tableswitch vs lookupswitch?
seniorScenario.answer: JVM chooses; large string switches use hash-based strategies.

seniorScenario.question: Legal/compliance logging switch on country?
seniorScenario.answer: Centralize rules; test exhaustively when enum grows.

wrongAnswers: Switch expressions can fall through like classic switch without care.
Pattern matching removes the need for equals/hashCode.
You can switch on float primitive directly.
default case is optional in all switch expressions.
instanceof pattern variables are in scope after the if block unconditionally.
Switch on null String is silently ignored.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a thread in Java?
conceptual.answer: Independent path of execution; shares heap with other threads in same process.

conceptual.question: What is synchronized?
conceptual.answer: Intrinsic lock on object—mutual exclusion for critical sections.

conceptual.question: What is volatile?
conceptual.answer: Visibility guarantee for reads/writes—not compound atomicity for i++.

conceptual.question: What is java.util.concurrent?
conceptual.answer: Higher-level utilities: ExecutorService, ConcurrentHashMap, locks, atomics.

conceptual.question: What is ExecutorService?
conceptual.answer: Thread pool abstraction—submit tasks, manage lifecycle.

conceptual.question: What is Callable vs Runnable?
conceptual.answer: Callable returns value and throws checked exceptions—use with Future.

conceptual.question: What is CountDownLatch/CyclicBarrier?
conceptual.answer: Coordination primitives for threads meeting at points.

conceptual.question: What is deadlock?
conceptual.answer: Circular wait for locks—prevent with lock ordering and timeouts.

conceptual.question: What is race condition?
conceptual.answer: Outcome depends on timing—unsynchronized shared mutable state.

conceptual.question: What is AtomicInteger?
conceptual.answer: CAS-based atomic updates without synchronized for single variable.

conceptual.question: What is ReentrantLock?
conceptual.answer: Explicit lock with tryLock, fairness option—more flexible than synchronized.

conceptual.question: What is thread pool sizing heuristic?
conceptual.answer: CPU-bound ~ cores; IO-bound higher—measure.

conceptual.question: What is ForkJoinPool?
conceptual.answer: Work-stealing for divide-and-conquer parallel tasks.

conceptual.question: What is ThreadLocal?
conceptual.answer: Per-thread variable storage—use carefully to avoid leaks in pools.

conceptual.question: What is happens-before?
conceptual.answer: JMM rule establishing visibility ordering between actions.

codeBased.question: synchronized method vs block.
codeBased.answer: Lock object scope differs—use private final lock.

codeBased.question: volatile boolean flag visibility.
codeBased.answer: Reader thread sees writer update with volatile.

codeBased.question: ScheduledExecutorService scheduleAtFixedRate.
codeBased.answer: Periodic tasks—handle exceptions or task stops.

codeBased.question: ReadWriteLock read lock many writers exclusive.

seniorScenario.question: Thread pool task queue unbounded—risk?
seniorScenario.answer: OOM from queued tasks; use bounded queues and back-pressure.

seniorScenario.question: ThreadLocal in pooled threads—leak?
seniorScenario.answer: remove() in finally—especially in app servers.

seniorScenario.question: Debugging deadlock in prod?
seniorScenario.answer: Thread dump jstack; lock ordering; timeouts.

seniorScenario.question: synchronized on Integer—bad?
seniorScenario.answer: Integer cached objects—unrelated locks; use dedicated Object.

seniorScenario.question: Virtual threads vs platform threads—when?
seniorScenario.answer: IO-bound scalability—Java 21+; CPU-bound still need cores.

seniorScenario.question: Distributed locking vs in-JVM locks?
seniorScenario.answer: Redis/etcd locks for cluster coordination—different failure modes.

wrongAnswers: volatile makes i++ atomic on an int field.
synchronized blocks can never deadlock.
Creating a new thread per request is always the best scalability approach.
ConcurrentHashMap allows null keys like HashMap.
ExecutorService automatically shuts down when the app exits.
Thread.sleep releases locks held by the thread.

conceptual.question: What is fairness in ReentrantLock?
conceptual.answer: Longest-waiting thread acquires next—reduces starvation but lowers throughput.

conceptual.question: What is StampedLock?
conceptual.answer: Optimistic read locking for read-heavy scenarios—tricky API.

conceptual.question: What is CompletableFuture?
conceptual.answer: Composable async tasks with thenApply, thenCombine, exception handling.

conceptual.question: What is blocking queue in producer-consumer?
conceptual.answer: put/take coordinate back-pressure—LinkedBlockingQueue etc.

conceptual.question: What is CopyOnWriteArrayList?
conceptual.answer: Copy-on-write for rare writes many reads—snapshot iterators.

conceptual.question: What is ExecutorService lifecycle?
conceptual.answer: shutdown vs shutdownNow—wait termination with awaitTermination.

conceptual.question: What is fork/join misuse?
conceptual.answer: Tiny tasks overhead—thresholds matter.

conceptual.question: What is CompletableFuture thread pool?
conceptual.answer: Common ForkJoinPool or custom executor—specify for production control.

conceptual.question: What is parallelStream common pool?
conceptual.answer: Shared—can saturate; dangerous for blocking tasks.

conceptual.question: What is lock striping?
conceptual.answer: Split lock scope to reduce contention—ConcurrentHashMap segments historical.

conceptual.question: What is ABA problem?
conceptual.answer: CAS pitfalls in lock-free—AtomicStampedReference mitigates.

conceptual.question: What is LongAdder?
conceptual.answer: Scalable counters under contention—cells reduce CAS failures.

conceptual.question: What is Phaser?
conceptual.answer: Flexible barrier for dynamic number of parties—advanced.

conceptual.question: What is Exchanger?
conceptual.answer: Two threads swap objects at rendezvous point—niche.

codeBased.question: handle whenComplete exception.
codeBased.answer: Recover or log both success and failure.

codeBased.question: StampedLock optimistic read validate.
codeBased.answer: validate(stamp) after read—fallback to read lock.

codeBased.question: LongAdder sum.
codeBased.answer: sum() aggregation—eventual for high contention counters.

seniorScenario.question: CompletableFuture.get blocks—async lost?
seniorScenario.answer: Use thenAccept async callbacks or compose without blocking event threads.

seniorScenario.question: Common pool exhaustion from blocking CF?
seniorScenario.answer: Use custom executor for blocking IO tasks.

seniorScenario.question: Metrics for thread pool queue depth?
seniorScenario.answer: Alert before OOM; tune max pool and queue.

seniorScenario.question: Distributed transaction vs local locks?
seniorScenario.answer: Different consistency—don’t confuse JDBC transaction with Lock.

seniorScenario.question: Reactive vs CompletableFuture style?
seniorScenario.answer: Different ecosystems—interop carefully.

seniorScenario.question: GC pauses vs low-latency locks?
seniorScenario.answer: Consider Shenandoah/ZGC and avoid full STW phases in SLA paths.

wrongAnswers: ReentrantLock automatically releases when the method returns like synchronized.
CompletableFuture always runs on a new thread for each stage.
CopyOnWriteArrayList is best for write-heavy workloads.
StampedLock is the simplest lock for beginners.
LongAdder is always faster than AtomicLong for every use case.
shutdownNow guarantees all tasks completed successfully.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is CompletableFuture?
conceptual.answer: Non-blocking composition of async tasks with callbacks and combining futures.

conceptual.question: What is thenApply vs thenCompose?
conceptual.answer: thenApply maps value; thenCompose flattens CompletableFuture-returning function.

conceptual.question: What is ForkJoinPool.commonPool?
conceptual.answer: Shared pool for parallel streams and default async executor—limited threads.

conceptual.question: What are virtual threads (Java 21)?
conceptual.answer: Lightweight threads mapped to carrier threads—great for blocking IO at huge scale.

conceptual.question: What is structured concurrency (preview)?
conceptual.answer: Scopes child tasks lifetime—fail together—emerging pattern.

conceptual.question: What is executor choice for CF?
conceptual.answer: Specify Executor for blocking work—don’t block common pool.

conceptual.question: What is exceptionally / handle?
conceptual.answer: Recover from failures in pipeline—typed handling.

conceptual.question: What is orTimeout / completeOnTimeout (Java 9+)?
conceptual.answer: Bound wait times—avoid hanging futures.

conceptual.question: What is join vs get on CompletableFuture?
conceptual.answer: join unchecked; get throws checked ExecutionException.

conceptual.question: What is runAsync vs supplyAsync?
conceptual.answer: No return vs Supplier return value.

conceptual.question: Virtual thread pinning issue?
conceptual.answer: Synchronized block or native code can pin to carrier—use reentrant locks or avoid.

conceptual.question: What is carrier thread?
conceptual.answer: OS thread backing many virtual threads—scheduling layer.

conceptual.question: Platform thread cost?
conceptual.answer: Megabytes stack vs virtual kilobytes—scalability difference.

conceptual.question: What is CompletableFuture delayedExecutor?
conceptual.answer: Schedule tasks with delay—Java 9+.

conceptual.question: Thread-per-request vs virtual threads for servlet?
conceptual.answer: Virtual threads improve throughput under blocking workloads.

seniorScenario.question: Outage: thread pool saturated—symptoms?
seniorScenario.answer: Latency spikes, rejected executions—metrics on active count and queue.

seniorScenario.question: Blocking call inside CF common pool—impact?
seniorScenario.answer: Starves parallel streams and other async—dedicate pool.

seniorScenario.question: Distributed trace across CF stages?
seniorScenario.answer: Propagate trace context manually or with framework support.

seniorScenario.question: Virtual threads and JDBC drivers?
seniorScenario.answer: Ensure drivers release pinning; monitor thread dumps.

seniorScenario.question: Choosing CompletableFuture vs Project Reactor?
seniorScenario.answer: Ecosystem integration—Spring WebFlux uses Reactor.

seniorScenario.question: Cancellation of CompletableFuture chains?
seniorScenario.answer: cancel may not stop running task—cooperative cancellation.

wrongAnswers: CompletableFuture.get never blocks the calling thread.
Virtual threads replace the need for thread pools entirely.
thenApply runs on the same thread that completed the previous stage always.
ForkJoinPool.commonPool has unlimited threads.
CompletableFuture cannot complete exceptionally.
join() throws checked InterruptedException like get().

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is JVM heap?
conceptual.answer: Runtime data area for object allocation—Garbage Collector manages reclamation.

conceptual.question: What is stack vs heap?
conceptual.answer: Stack per-thread frames with local vars; heap shared objects—escape analysis may stack-allocate.

conceptual.question: What is GC root?
conceptual.answer: Reachability starting points—threads, static fields, JNI refs.

conceptual.question: What is generational hypothesis?
conceptual.answer: Most objects die young—Young gen collections frequent, Old gen less.

conceptual.question: What is Stop-The-World pause?
conceptual.answer: Some GC phases halt application threads—latency sensitive apps care.

conceptual.question: What is G1 GC?
conceptual.answer: Region-based, targets pause time goals—common default in modern JDK.

conceptual.question: What is ZGC / Shenandoah?
conceptual.answer: Low-latency concurrent collectors—large heaps, sub-ms goals under conditions.

conceptual.question: What is Metaspace?
conceptual.answer: Class metadata since Java 8—replaces PermGen; unbounded unless MaxMetaspaceSize.

conceptual.question: What is JIT compilation tiers?
conceptual.answer: C1 fast compile, C2 optimized—tiered compilation balances startup vs peak.

conceptual.question: What is class loading delegation?
conceptual.answer: Parent-first model—security and consistency.

conceptual.question: What is -Xmx -Xms?
conceptual.answer: Max and initial heap—sizing for containers important.

conceptual.question: What is Native Memory Tracking?
conceptual.answer: Diagnose native leaks outside heap—JNI, metaspace, thread stacks.

conceptual.question: What is safepoint?
conceptual.answer: Points where threads pause for GC/deoptimization coordination.

conceptual.question: What is heap dump?
conceptual.answer: Snapshot for MAT analysis—find leaks retaining objects.

conceptual.question: What is container-aware ergonomics?
conceptual.answer: JDK reads cgroup limits to set default heap—avoid OOMKill mismatch.

codeBased.question: Trigger System.gc()—should you?
codeBased.answer: Hint only—not guaranteed; don’t rely in prod logic.

codeBased.question: Object creation in tight loop microbench sketch.
codeBased.answer: Measure allocation rate and GC impact.

codeBased.question: StackOverflowError vs OOM.
codeBased.answer: Stack depth vs heap space—different fixes.

codeBased.question: WeakReference use case.
codeBased.answer: Caches that should not prevent GC of referent.

seniorScenario.question: K8s pod OOMKilled but heap low?
seniorScenario.answer: Check native memory, direct buffers, thread stacks, metaspace.

seniorScenario.question: GC pause SLA breach after upgrade?
seniorScenario.answer: Review collector changes defaults; tune pause goal; profile allocation.

seniorScenario.question: Large heap Full GC storms?
seniorScenario.answer: Tune region sizes, humongous objects G1, avoid allocation spikes.

seniorScenario.question: Classloader leak in redeploys?
seniorScenario.answer: Metaspace growth—unload loaders properly; framework bugs.

seniorScenario.question: JVM flags from multiple sources conflict?
seniorScenario.answer: Args file, JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS, container—order of precedence.

seniorScenario.question: Choosing ZGC vs G1?
seniorScenario.answer: Latency vs throughput trade-offs—benchmark representative workloads.

wrongAnswers: Garbage collection runs only when System.gc() is called.
Stack memory holds all Java objects.
Metaspace is fixed size and never grows.
More heap always reduces GC time.
The JIT compiles all bytecode to native code before any execution.
WeakReference prevents an object from ever being garbage collected.
